pr.title: More splits based on process of elimination
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly-only reorganization that split additional code ranges out of the generic asm/melee/text_N.s buckets into named subsystem paths. The PR added asm/melee/lb/code_8000E9F0.s and asm/melee/cm/code_80028B9C.s, rewired obj_files.mk to build those new objects, shifted the remaining generic text buckets down, and deleted text_4.s after its contents were absorbed into text_3.s. There was no PR body or review discussion, so the stated rationale is limited to the title: these splits were made by process of elimination.
what_changed: Added asm/melee/lb/code_8000E9F0.s as a new assembly object containing a large code range beginning at func_8000E9F0 and ending in the excerpt near address 80028B98.
Added asm/melee/cm/code_80028B9C.s as a new assembly object containing a code range beginning at func_80028B9C and, from the diff hunk, extending through address 80031720.
Updated TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk to insert $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/lb/code_8000E9F0.o after lbvector.o.
Updated TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk to insert $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/cm/code_80028B9C.o before the existing pl/player.o and pl/code_8003715C.o entries.
Removed the old early generic $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/text_1.o entry from the top of TEXT_O_FILES because the newly split lb/cm objects now occupy that address range.
Renamed/rebased the remaining fallback buckets in obj_files.mk: old text_2.o became text_1.o, old text_3.o became text_2.o, and old text_4.o became text_3.o.
Deleted asm/melee/text_4.s and moved its remaining fallback assembly content into asm/melee/text_3.s.
The diff is dominated by movement of raw assembly between files rather than decompiled C or semantic source edits.
smart_moves: Kept object ordering explicit in obj_files.mk while splitting new address ranges; this is essential for preserving link order in an assembly-split decomp repository.

Named the new split files by subsystem directory plus starting address, code_8000E9F0.s under lb and code_80028B9C.s under cm, matching the repository's existing convention for unknown or not-yet-decompiled assembly objects.
Reduced reliance on generic text_N.s buckets by extracting two identified subsystem ranges, making later per-object decompilation and symbol naming easier.
After extracting new early ranges, compacted the remaining generic text_N.s sequence instead of leaving a deleted text_4.s placeholder in obj_files.mk.
decomp_lessons: When exact module identity is not fully known, a pragmatic split can still move progress forward by isolating address ranges into subsystem directories using start-address filenames.
Assembly split PRs should always update the object list at the same time as file movement; otherwise the binary link order can silently change or fail.

Large text bucket reshuffles may appear as enormous add/delete diffs even when the intended change is only object-boundary movement. Future reviewers and agents should inspect obj_files.mk and file start/end addresses before assuming semantic code changes.
The title indicates a useful early-project tactic: use process of elimination from surrounding known objects to assign remaining unknown assembly ranges to likely modules.
Generic text_N.s buckets are transitional artifacts; progressively replacing them with subsystem-local code_XXXXXXXX.s files improves searchability and future matching work.
naming_conventions: Unknown assembly object filenames use the pattern code_XXXXXXXX.s, where XXXXXXXX is the starting virtual address in uppercase hex, as seen in code_8000E9F0.s and code_80028B9C.s.
Subsystem directories are used even for not-yet-decompiled assembly splits, e.g. asm/melee/lb/ and asm/melee/cm/.
Generic fallback assembly buckets use sequential names text_1.s, text_2.s, text_3.s; this PR removed text_4.s and shifted the remaining buckets down.
Unidentified functions and labels in the assembly retain func_XXXXXXXX and lbl_XXXXXXXX names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split raw assembly at object boundaries inferred by process of elimination rather than by completed C matching.
Preserved assembly syntax with .include "macros.inc" and .section .text in the new split files.
Maintained start-address-based object naming to document the exact boundary of each extracted range.
Adjusted TEXT_O_FILES ordering to place new lb and cm objects before later known pl, mp, ef, ft, gm, gr, Runtime, and other objects.
Used whole-file movement of generic text bucket contents, causing text_2 to become text_1, text_3 to become text_2, and text_4 to become text_3.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes are available; conclusions are based on the title, changed files, and obj_files.mk diff.
searchable_terms: More splits based on process of elimination
follow_up_queries: Which later PRs decompile or rename functions from asm/melee/lb/code_8000E9F0.s?
Which later PRs decompile or rename functions from asm/melee/cm/code_80028B9C.s?
Find subsequent obj_files.mk changes that replace generic text_1.s, text_2.s, or text_3.s with subsystem-specific objects.
Look for later comments or commits explaining why the 80028B9C range belongs under cm.
